cards.title: Ageing drive-cycle scope and campaign role

citation.ieee_reference: [M14] "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," v3.0, "Drive cycle," pp. 54-57.
citation.title: Drive cycle
citation.linked_reference_markdown: [M14] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," White Paper, 2018, pp. 54-57.

key_takeaways: The chapter frames drive-cycle ageing as a more application-like alternative to constant-current cycle-life testing.
Capacity, resistance, and power capability are the checkpoint observables it calls out explicitly.
limitations: The exact profile family still depends on the application and standard being targeted.
allowed_deviations: Profile-family and checkpoint-cadence changes require review.

cards.title: Ageing drive-cycle execution loop

citation.ieee_reference: [M14] "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," v3.0, "Drive cycle," pp. 54-57.
citation.title: Drive cycle
citation.linked_reference_markdown: [M14] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," White Paper, 2018, pp. 54-57.

key_takeaways: The chapter describes a loop of initial check-up, repeated ageing blocks, intermediate characterization, and continue-or-stop decisions.
It explicitly allows reduced intermediate characterization to save time, but not at the expense of the core observables.
limitations: The breakpoint cadence is intentionally left application-dependent.
allowed_deviations: Reduced check-up scope must still preserve capacity, resistance, and power-capability tracking.

cards.title: Ageing drive-cycle outputs

citation.ieee_reference: [M14] "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," v3.0, "Drive cycle," pp. 54-57.
citation.title: Drive cycle
citation.linked_reference_markdown: [M14] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," White Paper, 2018, pp. 54-57.

key_takeaways: The chapter expects capacity and resistance trends over cycle life or equivalent throughput.
The end-of-life decision may be capacity-, resistance-, time-, or resource-limited.
limitations: The chapter does not prescribe a single reporting KPI hierarchy.
allowed_deviations: If an alternative end criterion is chosen, keep it explicit in the output.

cards.title: Calendar-ageing scope and planning role

citation.ieee_reference: [M15] "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," v3.0, "Calendar ageing test (battery under rest)," pp. 58-61.
citation.title: Calendar ageing test (battery under rest)
citation.linked_reference_markdown: [M15] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," White Paper, 2018, pp. 58-61.

key_takeaways: The chapter frames calendar ageing as storage at controlled SOC and temperature with periodic 25 C check-ups.
Charge retention and self-discharge are explicitly named as derived outputs.
limitations: The final experience plan still depends on the intended application and batch size.
allowed_deviations: Storage-matrix breadth and periodicity may move only after review.

cards.title: Calendar-ageing execution loop

citation.ieee_reference: [M15] "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," v3.0, "Calendar ageing test (battery under rest)," pp. 58-61.
citation.title: Calendar ageing test (battery under rest)
citation.linked_reference_markdown: [M15] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," White Paper, 2018, pp. 58-61.

key_takeaways: The execution loop is 25 C check-up, target-SOC setting, storage block, return to 25 C, residual discharge, then repeat.
The chapter provides temperature-dependent example periodicities and suggests adapting them to keep irreversible loss between check-ups small.
limitations: The exact reference-capacity definition can vary across projects.
allowed_deviations: Do not remove the 25 C check-up loop without an explicit review note.

cards.title: Calendar-ageing outputs and stop criteria

citation.ieee_reference: [M15] "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," v3.0, "Calendar ageing test (battery under rest)," pp. 58-61.
citation.title: Calendar ageing test (battery under rest)
citation.linked_reference_markdown: [M15] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," White Paper, 2018, pp. 58-61.

key_takeaways: The chapter expects capacity and resistance evolution versus storage duration, plus charge retention and self-discharge when needed.
Stop criteria include duration, SOH threshold, and visible mechanical degradation.
limitations: The chapter does not force a single SOH definition beyond recommending capacity-based default thresholds.
allowed_deviations: If a non-capacity SOH definition is used, preserve that choice explicitly.

cards.title: Constant-voltage ageing evidence gap

citation.ieee_reference: [M16] "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," v3.0, "Constant voltage test (battery maintained at a certain voltage)," p. 62.
citation.title: Constant voltage test (battery maintained at a certain voltage)
citation.linked_reference_markdown: [M16] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," White Paper, 2018, p. 62.

key_takeaways: The extracted chapter only preserves the heading and a preferable-test-schemes stub.
The current handbook extraction does not provide an executable procedure.
limitations: Any executable plan still needs a reviewed SOP or stronger literature-backed addendum.
allowed_deviations: No execution should be released from this card alone.

key_takeaways: The current execution block in the registry is intentionally marked as a placeholder for reviewed SOP completion.
Voltage, temperature, and hold duration must therefore stay explicit review items.

cards.title: Constant-voltage ageing placeholder outputs

citation.ieee_reference: [M16] "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," v3.0, "Constant voltage test (battery maintained at a certain voltage)," p. 62.
citation.title: Constant voltage test (battery maintained at a certain voltage)
citation.linked_reference_markdown: [M16] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," White Paper, 2018, p. 62.

key_takeaways: The current output family is a planning scaffold: hold-current drift plus checkpoint RPT trends.
The asset exists so the system can keep the method visible without pretending the chapter is complete.
limitations: No report should treat these outputs as an approved handbook claim.
allowed_deviations: Preserve the evidence-gap warning in any downstream output.
